reviews.review_description:
Honestly, this is awesome. I love the Oculus Go and some of the more experimental stuff on it, and this is a great example of that. This is like a Disney “dark ride,” with scripted events happening on rails.

Four pixies carry you through the air (at nighttime it’s actually four imps), and you fly through a dark cave and into a medieval cliffside town to explore. By day the town is active and merry, and by night there are various creeping terrors. I have a phobia of stuff moving underwater, so I was decently terrified when the fairies/imps dropped my basket into a lake like I was Moses.

I have no issues paying us$2 for a premium ride, especially one I can potentially experience again and again. I’d love to see more value-priced capsule experiences like this in the store.

I wasn’t sure what to expect from this experience, but I was pleasantly charmed. I like the light experience, and I would love it if it went longer. That being said I’m perfectly happy with it the way it is… I’ve showed it to friends and they had the same reaction I did. Definitely worth trying to experience for yourself! Lovely. Transporting. ￼

reviews.review_title: Everything you like & don't from Polyraw
reviews.review_description:
This company does some things so uniquely and so well, I have great respect for their content. This is basically a mashup of their previous works. Audio is always amazing, some segments of their graphics are always top shelf, and willing to take you at a slow pace so you can enjoy the trip. I LOVE that the day and night are totally different,  like 2 full rides.

I don't like that there is a hesitation and skip every time a new animation begins. It breaks immersion badly. I'm on an S10e, so very fast processor, but it's a problem that has always existed with this company. There is very apparent rendering as you move forward where fuzzy distant things suddenly become rendered. Also breaks immersion badly, also a classic Polyraw issue- other companies do this much better. Finally, some content just recycled from previous apps, why? I'll recommend it like I do all their stuff,  but wish they could put together one flawless experience, it would be a true treat.
It’s fun, cute, offers different choices, well worth trying!

Fantastic job! Looks great on the Oculus Go! And I am a major fan of your other application Relax River. You must release another! 

Warmest regards.
wish both journeys day/night were longer and add a music on/off option.
Is a small journey, in a small town, beautiful atmosphere, I did like the end when the butterflies are over the water.

It's so relaxing I watch it over and over  again  I just wish it was  longer  , I would have paid more if it longer cos it's worth it  , the graphics are brilliant  , plus I love the flying part as you come out the cave   it would have been great if that was longer  the first track  and especially the second track are so lovely  it makes me want to keep watching it  and watching the fairys  holding the basket they are so realistic  .
Keep up the good work
Super relaxing love it wish they would change it up a little bit though give it some variety but I still gave all the stars because it’s really cool actually
Very peaceful and relaxing.
I thought this app is fantastic

Two VR journeys, one by day the other by night introduce you to the denizens in and around a fantasy medieval village. You can look around but not walk around as you are conveyed on your journey in a crate. Its a pretty good experience and well worth the price of admission.
The fairy ride was ok but the gargoyle ride felt creepy so I couldn't even go through it . Not worth my time.
Great follow up to relaxing river...please more of these type of gentle rides..it’s what VR was invented for..😁👏

One day while just randomly browsing through the vast Go library, I just so happened to stumble upon the realization that Polyraw was the developer responsible for what looked like three pretty cool experiences: Terror Cave, Relax River, and Dream Journey. Considering that all three of these titles were reasonably priced, I just went ahead and purchased them all sight unseen. Almost like a trilogy, if you will. As such, this review will act as one part of a three-part review.

So, this is definitely the title that Polyraw has been working towards. It is the last of the three to be released, and the quality shows. It combines all of the best elements from the previous two into a fully-realized experience that excels in all categories. The concept and art design choices are highly imaginative. The graphics are top-notch. The movement is fluid with no bugs. The audio and music is varied and perfectly matches the shifts in tone and setting. Just like Relax River, Dream Journey offers both a daytime and nighttime mode and the experience is profoundly different between the two. This is an excellent showcase for the Oculus Go, and has the most replay value amongst the three. If you only get one of them, this is definitely it. Enjoy the journey.

developer_response.body: We wish to say thank you for the appreciative and detailed reviews of our products, always an incentive to us to keep developing on the same line, always better and improving.Best regards.

reviews.review_description: Absolutely brilliant....please make more!

Such a well thought out, beautiful experience. Would love to see more from these developers!!!
I’m hooked!
Absolutely beautiful experience, wish it was longer. Hope the developers make more.
I love this app! Both trips are surreal and enchanting and full of surprises. Please make some more!
I really enjoyed this app, scenic routes were very relaxing.  I can take this ride a few times in a day.  Great job developers 👍
